quiz.title: Pre-calculus: Unit 2 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: 2.1: Relations vs. Functions
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Identifying functions, relations, and the Vertical Line Test

questions.question: Which of the following defines a function?
options: Every output corresponds to exactly one input.
Every x-value has at least two y-values.
Every input corresponds to exactly one output.
The graph passes the horizontal line test.
questions.explanation: A function is a mathematical rule where each element in the domain (input) is paired with exactly one element in the range (output).

questions.question: Which set of ordered pairs represents a function?
questions.explanation: In this set, every x-value is unique. In the other options, at least one x-value repeats with a different y-value, violating the function definition.

questions.question: If a graph fails the Vertical Line Test, what does that conclude about the relation?
options: The relation is not a function.
The function is not linear.
The domain is all real numbers.
The graph has no y-intercept.
questions.explanation: Failing the Vertical Line Test means a single x-value is associated with multiple y-values, which means the relation cannot be a function.

questions.question: Is the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 9$ a function of $x$?
options: Yes, because it is a common geometric shape.
No, because $x$ can never be negative.
Yes, because every $x$ has exactly one $y$.
No, because it is a circle and fails the VLT.
questions.explanation: Solving for $y$ yields $y = \pm \sqrt{9 - x^2}$. Since most $x$ values result in two different $y$ values, it fails the Vertical Line Test.

questions.question: In the mapping of a function, is it permissible for two different inputs to share the same output?
options: No, that would violate the definition of a function.
Only if the function is a straight line.
Only if the output is zero.
questions.explanation: Functions can be 'many-to-one.' As long as each individual input points to only one output, the definition of a function is satisfied.

questions.question: Which of these graphs represents a function?
options: A circle centered at the origin.
A vertical line.
A parabola opening to the right.
A parabola opening upward.
questions.explanation: An upward-opening parabola passes the Vertical Line Test. Circles, vertical lines, and sideways parabolas all fail the test.

questions.question: The set of all possible input values for a function is known as the:
options: Range
Domain
Codomain
Relation
questions.explanation: The domain is the collection of all independent values ($x$) for which the function is defined.

questions.question: If $f(x) = 5$ for every value of $x$, is $f$ a function?
options: Yes, it is a constant function.
No, because the y-value repeats.
No, because it has no slope.
Yes, because it is a vertical line.
questions.explanation: This represents a horizontal line. It passes the Vertical Line Test because every input $x$ corresponds to exactly one output (5).

questions.question: Which of the following describes a relation that is NOT a function?
options: A person's height mapped to their age.
A person's age mapped to their height.
A car's VIN number mapped to its owner.
A student's ID number mapped to their GPA.
questions.explanation: If age is the input and height is the output, it is a function (one age = one height). If height is the input, many people (different ages) can have the same height, making it a non-function mapping in reverse.

questions.question: The Vertical Line Test identifies functions. What does the Horizontal Line Test identify?
options: If a function is one-to-one (has an inverse).
If a function is linear.
If the function has a y-intercept.
If the domain is restricted.
questions.explanation: The Horizontal Line Test determines if a function is one-to-one, which is a requirement for a function to have an inverse function.

scoreMessages.90: Great start to Unit 2! You've got the definitions down.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Remember: x cannot repeat, but y can.
scoreMessages.50: Review the Vertical Line Test and the definition of a function.
scoreMessages.0: Make sure you understand the difference between an input and an output.
